title: impossible to start a stopped container
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. stop container 1: `podman stop drone`

2. stop container 2: `podman stop bitwarden`

3. start again each container: `podman start drone && podman start bitwarden`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: unable to start container "unruffled_goldberg": container_linux.go:338: creating new parent process caused "container_linux.go:1913: running lstat on namespace path \"/run/user/1000/netns/cni-eec32aae-bd8a-abb5-5be8-0e560fd6b4a5\" caused \"lstat /run/user/1000/netns/cni-eec32aae-bd8a-abb5-5be8-0e560fd6b4a5: no such file or directory\"": OCI runtime command not found error

Error: unable to start container "drone": container_linux.go:338: creating new parent process caused "container_linux.go:1913: running lstat on namespace path \"/run/user/1000/netns/cni-d6d06d6e-8553-812f-bcfe-8c4019f6d68e\" caused \"lstat /run/user/1000/netns/cni-d6d06d6e-8553-812f-bcfe-8c4019f6d68e: no such file or directory\"": OCI runtime command not found error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected that podman restart successfully each container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This happen each time, it's impossible to stop and after start again a container, I need to rm and re-create it

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman/bionic,now 1.6.2-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical server
Distributor ID: Debian
Description:    Debian GNU/Linux 10 (buster)
Release:        10
Codename:       buster

Repository used: ```deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu bionic main```

comments.body:
After an update of podman to 1.6.2-1, I obtain:

```
Error: unable to start container "drone": container create failed (no logs from conmon): EOF
```

When I try to start with the same command line `podman start bitwarden`

Any details on the two containers in question - what commands were used to create them, anything unusual about them?

Can you add `--log-level=debug` to your `podman start` commands and provide the output, as well as anything in syslog from `conmon`?

Sorry, I'll recreated containers but I'll do the same in test env, tomorrow, using same flags

Nothing about conmon in logs

```
podman run -d \
        --restart always \
        --name bitwarden \
        -v /var/lib/bitwardenrs-data/:/data \
        -e DOMAIN=https://vault.domain.tld \
        -e ADMIN_TOKEN=MY_ADMIN_TOKEN \
        -e WEBSOCKET_ENABLED=true \
        -p 127.0.0.1:3012:3012 \
        -e ROCKET_PORT=9001 \
        -p 127.0.0.1:9001:9001 \
        bitwardenrs/server:latest
```

```
podman run -d \
        -v /var/lib/drone:/data \
        -e DRONE_GIT_ALWAYS_AUTH=false \
        -e DRONE_GITEA_SERVER=https://git.domain.tld \
        -e DRONE_GITEA_CLIENT_ID=MY_UUID \
        -e DRONE_GITEA_CLIENT_SECRET=MY_SECRET \
        -e DRONE_SERVER_HOST=drone.domain.tld \
        -e DRONE_SERVER_PROTO=https \
        -e DRONE_TLS_AUTOCERT=false \
        -e DRONE_RPC_SECRET=MY_RPC_SECRET \
        -e DRONE_AGENTS_ENABLED=true \
        -e DRONE_CRON_INTERVAL=1m \
        -e DRONE_DATABASE_SECRET=MY_DB_PASSWORD \
        -e DRONE_USER_CREATE=username:my_username,admin:true \
        -e DRONE_USER_FILTER=my_username \
        -e DRONE_ADMIN=my_username \
        -p 127.0.0.1:20080:80 \
        -p 127.0.0.1:20443:443 \
        --restart=always \
        --name=drone \
        drone/drone
```

After the `--log-level=debug` run you should now have Conmon logs in syslog, can you provide those? The underlying error should be reported there

```
8232:Jan  6 16:49:38 sofia conmon: conmon 7d055e16ec437c397a22 <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied#012

8244:Jan  6 16:49:50 sofia conmon: conmon 7d055e16ec437c397a22 <error>: Failed to mkfifo at /home/colundrum/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/7d055e16ec437c397a22051c791c0cb422ee2a945b68c470a5d956687afaeccd/userdata/winsz: File exists
```

ah, version 1.6.2 of podman is a bit out of date for conmon 2.0.3. We need podman 1.6.4 for it to work seamlessly. A temp work around that isn't great is you can manually delete that winsz file between stops and starts.

@haircommander but I use ppa of projectatomic … no new version.
Isn't my fault if this ppa isn't up-to-date with correct version between packages. Have you a correct setup for me to update them?
you are very correct, and thanks for bringing our attention to it :smile: @lsm5 @jnovy PTAL

Any updates on this? I am facing the exact same issue and ppa still seems to be carrying version 1.6.2. Also noticed there's a [snap version of podman](https://snapcraft.io/podman), would that be better to use (seeing that it was updated recently)?
I believe that @lsm5 has been making some packaging changes to the PPA related to using obs; @lsm5 any updates on getting 1.7.0 built there?

@rhatdan Thanks
@ParitoshBh you can look directly at: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/d9bcf20b3de0f2f085044b1724154ba1950d46d6/install.md#debian
@colundrum Thanks. Followed the instructions, can confirm this particular issue has been resolved (version 1.7.0-rc1).

@ParitoshBh for me, I use stable and not testing so I've podman 1.6.4~2 and all fine
@rhatdan thanks
